---
title: "qPCR Host-DNA Depletion Analysis"
author: "Your Name"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: flatly
  pdf_document:
    toc:  true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6)

```


```{r load libraries}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(rstatix)
library(readxl)

```

#Import and organise data
```{r load data}
# Read the data
data <- read_excel("qPCR_host_depletion_chicken_all.xlsx")

# View structure
glimpse(data)

```


```{r clean data}

# Clean and structure the data
data_clean <- data %>%
  # Remove rows with all NA values
  filter(! is.na(Name)) %>%
  # Extract sample information from Name column
  mutate(
    # Determine if host depletion was applied
    Treatment = case_when(
      str_detect(Name, "No Host") ~ "Before Depletion",
      str_detect(Name, "Host-DNA Depletion|\\bHost\\b") ~ "After Depletion",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    # Make Treatment a factor with Before first
    Treatment = factor(Treatment, levels = c("Before Depletion", "After Depletion")),
    # Extract sample type
    Sample_Type = case_when(
      str_detect(Name, "cheesecloth") ~ "Cheesecloth",
      str_detect(Name, "RVS") ~ "RVS",
      str_detect(Name, "CAT") ~ "CAT",
      str_detect(Name, "Cent \\+ Filt") ~ "Centrifuge + Filter",
      str_detect(Name, "Filt") & ! str_detect(Name, "Cent") ~ "Filter Only",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    # Extract replicate number
    Replicate_ID = str_extract(Name, "\\d+$"),
    Replicate_ID = as.numeric(Replicate_ID)
  ) %>%
  # Keep only necessary columns
  select(Name, Treatment, Sample_Type, Replicate_ID, Ct, `Calc Conc (ng/ul)`) %>%
  # Rename for easier handling
  rename(Concentration = `Calc Conc (ng/ul)`)


# View cleaned data summary
data_clean %>% 
  group_by(Sample_Type, Treatment) %>% 
  summarise(
    Total_Measurements = n(),
    Detected = sum(!is.na(Concentration)),
    Non_Detects = sum(is.na(Concentration)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  knitr:: kable(caption = "Data Overview by Sample Type and Treatment")

```


```{r prepare data for plotting}

# Set detection limits for non-detects
# Non-detects indicate successful DNA depletion (below detection limit)

max_ct_observed <- max(data_clean$Ct, na.rm = TRUE)
detection_limit_ct <- 40  # Standard qPCR detection limit
detection_limit_conc <- 0.0001  # Very low concentration (0.1 pg/μL)

cat("Maximum Ct observed:", round(max_ct_observed, 2), "\n")
cat("Detection limit Ct:", detection_limit_ct, "\n")
cat("Detection limit concentration:", detection_limit_conc, "ng/μL\n")

# Create complete dataset with imputed values for non-detects
data_complete <- data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    # Flag non-detects
    Below_Detection = is.na(Ct),
    # For statistical purposes, assign detection limit to non-detects
    Ct_imputed = ifelse(is.na(Ct), detection_limit_ct, Ct),
    Concentration_imputed = ifelse(is.na(Concentration), detection_limit_conc, Concentration),
    # Keep track of original values
    Concentration_original = Concentration,
    Ct_original = Ct
  )

# Calculate summary statistics by biological replicate
# This averages the 3 technical triplicates for each biological replicate
summary_stats <- data_complete %>%
  group_by(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    n_measurements = n(),
    n_detected = sum(!Below_Detection),
    n_non_detect = sum(Below_Detection),
    Mean_Ct = mean(Ct, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Ct = sd(Ct, na.rm = TRUE),
    SE_Ct = SD_Ct / sqrt(sum(!is.na(Ct))),
    Mean_Conc = mean(Concentration, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Conc = sd(Concentration, na.rm = TRUE),
    SE_Conc = SD_Conc / sqrt(sum(!is.na(Concentration))),
    Mean_Conc_imputed = mean(Concentration_imputed, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_Ct_imputed = mean(Ct_imputed, na.rm = TRUE),
    All_Non_Detect = all(Below_Detection),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Display summary table
summary_stats %>%
  select(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID, n_detected, n_non_detect, Mean_Ct, Mean_Conc) %>%
  knitr:: kable(digits = 2, caption = "Summary Statistics by Biological Replicate")


```

#Cheesecloth samples
```{r Ct Values for Cheesecloth run}

# Filter cheesecloth data
cheesecloth_summary <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth")

# Create Ct plot
plot_cheesecloth_ct <- ggplot(cheesecloth_summary, 
                              aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Ct, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.9), alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(Mean_Ct - SE_Ct, 0), ymax = Mean_Ct + SE_Ct),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Mean_Ct), size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~paste("Replicate", Replicate_ID), ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title = "Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy (Ct Values)",
    subtitle = "Higher Ct = Less chicken DNA detected",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Ct Value",
    fill = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

print(plot_cheesecloth_ct)
ggsave("cheesecloth_ct_values.png", plot_cheesecloth_ct, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

```


#Cheesecloth - concentration
```{r Concentration Values for Cheesecloth run}
# Create concentration plot
plot_cheesecloth_conc <- ggplot(cheesecloth_summary, 
                                aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.9), alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(Mean_Conc - SE_Conc, 0), ymax = Mean_Conc + SE_Conc),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Mean_Conc), size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~paste("Replicate", Replicate_ID), ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title = "Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy (Concentration)",
    subtitle = "Chicken DNA concentration before and after host-DNA depletion",
    x = NULL,
    y = "DNA Concentration (ng/μL)",
    fill = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

print(plot_cheesecloth_conc)

ggsave("cheesecloth_concentration.png", plot_cheesecloth_conc, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

```


#Filtered Samples
```{r Ct Values for Filtered Samples}

# Filter data for filtered samples
filtered_summary <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type %in% c("Centrifuge + Filter", "Filter Only"))

# Check if we need log scale (if max/min ratio > 100)
conc_range <- filtered_summary %>% filter(! is.na(Mean_Conc)) %>% pull(Mean_Conc)
use_log_filtered <- ifelse(length(conc_range) > 1 && max(conc_range)/min(conc_range) > 100, TRUE, FALSE)

cat("Using log scale for filtered samples:", use_log_filtered, "\n")

```


```{r Ct values for Filtered Samples}

# Create Ct plot
plot_filtered_ct <- ggplot(filtered_summary, 
                           aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Ct_imputed, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.9), alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(Mean_Ct_imputed - SE_Ct, 0), ymax = pmin(Mean_Ct_imputed + SE_Ct, 40)),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Mean_Ct_imputed), size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
  geom_text(data = filtered_summary %>% filter(All_Non_Detect),
            aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Ct_imputed - 2, label = "ND"),
            size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  facet_grid(Sample_Type ~ paste("Replicate", Replicate_ID)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1)), limits = c(0, 40)) +
  labs(
    title = "Filtered Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy (Ct Values)",
    subtitle = "Higher Ct = Less DNA; ND = No chicken DNA Detected",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Ct Value",
    fill = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

print(plot_filtered_ct)
ggsave("filtered_ct_values.png", plot_filtered_ct, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 600)

```


```{r Concentration Values for Filtered Samples}

# Create concentration plot (log scale if necessary)
if(use_log_filtered) {
  plot_filtered_conc <- ggplot(filtered_summary, 
                               aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc_imputed, fill = Treatment)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.9), alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(y = Mean_Conc_imputed), size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
    geom_text(data = filtered_summary %>% filter(All_Non_Detect),
              aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc_imputed * 3, label = "ND"),
              size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "#4DBBD5") +
    facet_grid(Sample_Type ~ paste("Replicate", Replicate_ID)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
    scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10",
                       labels = scales::trans_format("log10", scales:: math_format(10^.x))) +
    labs(
      title = "Filtered Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy (Concentration)",
      subtitle = "Higher Ct = Less DNA; ND = No chicken DNA Detected",
      x = NULL,
      y = "DNA Concentration (ng/μL, log scale)",
      fill = "Treatment"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
} else {
  plot_filtered_conc <- ggplot(filtered_summary, 
                               aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc_imputed, fill = Treatment)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.9), alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(Mean_Conc_imputed - SE_Conc, 0), 
                      ymax = Mean_Conc_imputed + SE_Conc),
                  width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
    geom_point(aes(y = Mean_Conc_imputed), size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
    geom_text(data = filtered_summary %>% filter(All_Non_Detect),
              aes(x = Treatment, y = max(filtered_summary$Mean_Conc_imputed, na.rm=TRUE) * 0.05, 
                  label = "ND"),
              size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "#4DBBD5") +
    facet_grid(Sample_Type ~ paste("Replicate", Replicate_ID)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
    labs(
      title = "Filtered Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy (Concentration)",
      subtitle = "Higher Ct = Less DNA; ND = No chicken DNA Detected",
      x = NULL,
      y = "DNA Concentration (ng/μL)",
      fill = "Treatment"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
}

print(plot_filtered_conc)
ggsave("filtered_concentration.png", plot_filtered_conc, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 600)

```

#Enrichment samples

```{r organise data for Enrichment Samples}
# Filter data for enriched samples (only keep replicates with at least some "Before" data)
enriched_summary <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type %in% c("RVS", "CAT")) %>%
  group_by(Sample_Type, Replicate_ID) %>%
  filter(any(Treatment == "Before Depletion" & n_detected > 0)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Check if we need log scale
conc_range_enriched <- enriched_summary %>% filter(!is.na(Mean_Conc)) %>% pull(Mean_Conc)
use_log_enriched <- ifelse(length(conc_range_enriched) > 1 && max(conc_range_enriched)/min(conc_range_enriched) > 100, TRUE, FALSE)

cat("Using log scale for enriched samples:", use_log_enriched, "\n")
```


```{r Ct Values for Enrichment Samples}

# Create Ct plot
plot_enriched_ct <- ggplot(enriched_summary, 
                           aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Ct_imputed, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.9), alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(Mean_Ct_imputed - SE_Ct, 0), ymax = pmin(Mean_Ct_imputed + SE_Ct, 40)),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Mean_Ct_imputed), size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
  geom_text(data = enriched_summary %>% filter(All_Non_Detect),
            aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Ct_imputed - 2, label = "ND"),
            size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  facet_grid(Sample_Type ~ paste("Replicate", Replicate_ID), scales = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1)), limits = c(0, 40)) +
  labs(
    title = "Enriched Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy (Ct Values)",
    subtitle = "RVS and CAT enrichment; Higher Ct = Less DNA; ND = No chicken DNA Detected",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Ct Value",
    fill = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

print(plot_enriched_ct)
ggsave("enriched_ct_values.png", plot_enriched_ct, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 600)

```

```{r Concentration Values for Enrichment Samples}

# Create concentration plot (log scale if necessary)
if(use_log_enriched) {
  plot_enriched_conc <- ggplot(enriched_summary, 
                               aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc_imputed, fill = Treatment)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.9), alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(y = Mean_Conc_imputed), size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
    geom_text(data = enriched_summary %>% filter(All_Non_Detect),
              aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc_imputed * 3, label = "ND"),
              size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "#4DBBD5") +
    facet_grid(Sample_Type ~ paste("Replicate", Replicate_ID), scales = "free_x") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
    scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10",
                       labels = scales::trans_format("log10", scales::math_format(10^.x))) +
    labs(
      title = "Enriched Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy (Concentration)",
      subtitle = "Log scale; ND = No chicken DNA detected (below detection limit)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "DNA Concentration (ng/μL, log scale)",
      fill = "Treatment"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
} else {
  plot_enriched_conc <- ggplot(enriched_summary, 
                               aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc_imputed, fill = Treatment)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.9), alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(Mean_Conc_imputed - SE_Conc, 0), 
                      ymax = Mean_Conc_imputed + SE_Conc),
                  width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
    geom_point(aes(y = Mean_Conc_imputed), size = 3, shape = 21, fill = "white") +
    geom_text(data = enriched_summary %>% filter(All_Non_Detect),
              aes(x = Treatment, y = max(enriched_summary$Mean_Conc_imputed, na.rm=TRUE) * 0.05, 
                  label = "ND"),
              size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "#4DBBD5") +
    facet_grid(Sample_Type ~ paste("Replicate", Replicate_ID), scales = "free_x") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
    labs(
      title = "Enriched Samples: Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy (Concentration)",
      subtitle = "ND = No chicken DNA detected (below detection limit)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "DNA Concentration (ng/μL)",
      fill = "Treatment"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
}

print(plot_enriched_conc)
ggsave("enriched_concentration.png", plot_enriched_conc, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 600)

```


#Statistical Analysis
```{r Statistical Analysis - t-test verify pairing}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("VERIFYING SAMPLE PAIRING\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("Experimental Design:\n")
cat("Each biological replicate (1, 2, 3) represents the SAME sample\n")
cat("measured before and after host-DNA depletion.\n\n")

cat("This is a PAIRED design, which is more powerful because:\n")
cat("  ✓ Controls for sample-to-sample variability\n")
cat("  ✓ Each sample serves as its own control\n")
cat("  ✓ Increases statistical power\n\n")

# Show the pairing structure for cheesecloth
pairing_check <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth", ! is.na(Mean_Conc)) %>%
  select(Replicate_ID, Treatment, Mean_Ct, Mean_Conc) %>%
  arrange(Replicate_ID, Treatment)

for(rep in unique(pairing_check$Replicate_ID)) {
  rep_data <- pairing_check %>% filter(Replicate_ID == rep)
  
  cat("Biological Replicate", rep, "(Same Sample):\n")
  
  before <- rep_data %>% filter(Treatment == "Before Depletion")
  after <- rep_data %>% filter(Treatment == "After Depletion")
  
  if(nrow(before) > 0) {
    cat("  Before:   ", round(before$Mean_Conc, 2), " ng/μL (Ct = ", 
        round(before$Mean_Ct, 2), ")\n", sep="")
  }
  
  if(nrow(after) > 0) {
    cat("  After:   ", round(after$Mean_Conc, 2), " ng/μL (Ct = ", 
        round(after$Mean_Ct, 2), ")\n", sep="")
  }
  
  if(nrow(before) > 0 && nrow(after) > 0) {
    fold_reduction <- before$Mean_Conc / after$Mean_Conc
    delta_ct <- after$Mean_Ct - before$Mean_Ct
    cat("  Change:   ", round(fold_reduction, 2), "× reduction (ΔCt = +", 
        round(delta_ct, 2), ")\n", sep="")
  }
  
  cat("\n")
}

cat("✓ Pairing verified!\n\n")

```


```{r visualise paired design}

# Create a plot that clearly shows the paired nature
pairing_data <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth", !is.na(Mean_Conc)) %>%
  mutate(Replicate_Label = paste("Sample", Replicate_ID))

plot_pairing <- ggplot(pairing_data, 
                       aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc, group = Replicate_ID)) +
  # Lines connecting paired samples
  geom_line(aes(color = Replicate_Label), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  # Points for each measurement
  geom_point(aes(fill = Treatment), size = 6, shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 1.5) +
  # Add labels for each sample
  geom_text(aes(label = Replicate_ID), size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", 
                               "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Sample 1" = "#00A087", 
                                "Sample 2" = "#3C5488", 
                                "Sample 3" = "#F39B7F"),
                     name = "Paired Samples") +
  labs(
    title = "Paired Sample Design:  Cheesecloth Host-DNA Depletion",
    subtitle = "Lines connect the SAME biological sample before and after treatment",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Chicken DNA Concentration (ng/μL)",
    caption = "Each colored line represents one biological sample measured before and after depletion"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

print(plot_pairing)
ggsave("cheesecloth_paired_design.png", plot_pairing, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```


```{r paired t-test cheesecloth}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("PAIRED T-TEST:  CHEESECLOTH SAMPLES\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Get the biological replicate means for cheesecloth
cheesecloth_data <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth", !is.na(Mean_Conc)) %>%
  select(Replicate_ID, Treatment, Mean_Conc, Mean_Ct)

# Reshape to wide format for paired test
cheesecloth_wide <- cheesecloth_data %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Treatment, values_from = c(Mean_Conc, Mean_Ct))

# Extract before and after values
before <- cheesecloth_wide$`Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`
after <- cheesecloth_wide$`Mean_Conc_After Depletion`

cat("Data for paired t-test:\n")
cat("Before Depletion:", before, "ng/μL\n")
cat("After Depletion:  ", after, "ng/μL\n\n")

# Perform paired t-test
paired_result <- t.test(before, after, paired = TRUE, alternative = "greater")

cat("========================================\n")
cat("PAIRED T-TEST RESULTS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("Null hypothesis (H₀):      No difference in DNA concentration\n")
cat("Alternative hypothesis (Hₐ): Before > After (depletion reduces DNA)\n\n")

cat("t-statistic:        ", round(paired_result$statistic, 4), "\n")
cat("Degrees of freedom:", paired_result$parameter, "\n")
cat("p-value:          ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 5), "\n")
cat("95% CI:            (", round(paired_result$conf.int[1], 2), ", Infinity)\n\n", sep="")

# Calculate descriptive statistics
mean_before <- mean(before)
mean_after <- mean(after)
sd_before <- sd(before)
sd_after <- sd(after)

differences <- before - after
mean_diff <- mean(differences)
sd_diff <- sd(differences)

cat("DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("Before Depletion:\n")
cat("  Mean:  ", round(mean_before, 2), " ng/μL\n")
cat("  SD:    ", round(sd_before, 2), " ng/μL\n\n")

cat("After Depletion:\n")
cat("  Mean:  ", round(mean_after, 4), " ng/μL\n")
cat("  SD:    ", round(sd_after, 4), " ng/μL\n\n")

cat("Difference (Before - After):\n")
cat("  Mean:  ", round(mean_diff, 2), " ng/μL\n")
cat("  SD:    ", round(sd_diff, 2), " ng/μL\n\n")

# Effect sizes
fold_change <- mean_before / mean_after
percent_reduction <- (mean_diff / mean_before) * 100
cohens_d <- mean_diff / sd_diff

cat("EFFECT SIZE:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("Fold reduction:    ", round(fold_change, 2), "×\n")
cat("Percent reduction: ", round(percent_reduction, 1), "%\n")
cat("Cohen's d:         ", round(cohens_d, 2), " (", 
    ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 1.2, "Very Large",
    ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.8, "Large",
    ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.5, "Medium", "Small"))), " effect)\n\n")

# Ct values
ct_before <- cheesecloth_wide$`Mean_Ct_Before Depletion`
ct_after <- cheesecloth_wide$`Mean_Ct_After Depletion`
delta_ct <- mean(ct_after - ct_before)

cat("Ct VALUES:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("Mean Ct Before:         ", round(mean(ct_before), 2), "\n")
cat("Mean Ct After:         ", round(mean(ct_after), 2), "\n")
cat("ΔCt (After - Before):  ", round(delta_ct, 2), "\n")
cat("(Higher ΔCt indicates more depletion)\n\n")

# Interpretation
cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
if(paired_result$p.value < 0.001) {
  cat("*** p < 0.001 - HIGHLY SIGNIFICANT\n")
  cat("Very strong evidence that depletion reduces chicken DNA.\n\n")
} else if(paired_result$p.value < 0.01) {
  cat("** p < 0.01 - VERY SIGNIFICANT\n")
  cat("Strong evidence that depletion reduces chicken DNA.\n\n")
} else if(paired_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("* p < 0.05 - SIGNIFICANT\n")
  cat("Evidence that depletion reduces chicken DNA.\n\n")
} else if(paired_result$p.value < 0.10) {
  cat("† p < 0.10 - MARGINALLY SIGNIFICANT / TREND\n")
  cat("Suggestive evidence that depletion reduces chicken DNA.\n")
  cat("The effect is likely real but limited by sample size (n=3).\n\n")
} else {
  cat("p ≥ 0.10 - NOT SIGNIFICANT\n")
  cat("Insufficient evidence at conventional significance levels.\n\n")
}

cat("========================================\n\n")

```


```{r visualisation with stats}

# Get overall means and CI
cheesecloth_overall <- cheesecloth_data %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(Mean_Conc),
    SD = sd(Mean_Conc),
    SE = SD / sqrt(n()),
    Lower_CI = Mean - qt(0.975, n()-1) * SE,
    Upper_CI = Mean + qt(0.975, n()-1) * SE,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Create detailed plot
plot_stats <- ggplot() +
  # Overall mean bars with 95% CI
  geom_col(data = cheesecloth_overall,
           aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean, fill = Treatment),
           alpha = 0.6, width = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbar(data = cheesecloth_overall,
                aes(x = Treatment, ymin = Lower_CI, ymax = Upper_CI),
                width = 0.15, size = 1.2) +
  # Individual biological replicates
  geom_point(data = cheesecloth_data,
             aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc, group = Replicate_ID),
             size = 5, shape = 21, fill = "white", stroke = 1.5) +
  # Connecting lines for paired data
  geom_line(data = cheesecloth_data,
            aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc, group = Replicate_ID),
            color = "gray40", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  # Add replicate labels
  geom_text(data = cheesecloth_data,
            aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean_Conc, label = Replicate_ID),
            size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  # Add statistics annotation
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 2.5, 
           ymin = max(cheesecloth_data$Mean_Conc) * 0.85,
           ymax = max(cheesecloth_data$Mean_Conc) * 1.05,
           fill = "white", color = "black", linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = max(cheesecloth_data$Mean_Conc) * 0.97,
           label = paste0("Paired t-test\n",
                         "p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 3), "\n",
                         round(fold_change, 1), "-fold reduction\n",
                         "Cohen's d = ", round(cohens_d, 2)),
           size = 4, fontface = "bold", lineheight = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Before Depletion" = "#E64B35", 
                               "After Depletion" = "#4DBBD5")) +
  labs(
    title = "Cheesecloth Samples:  Host-DNA Depletion Efficacy",
    subtitle = "Each biological replicate is the mean of 3 technical replicates | n=3",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Chicken DNA Concentration (ng/μL)",
    caption = "Bars = Mean ± 95% CI | Points = Individual biological replicates | Lines connect paired samples"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

print(plot_stats)
ggsave("cheesecloth_paired_ttest.png", plot_stats, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 600)


```


```{r check assumptions}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("CHECKING T-TEST ASSUMPTIONS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# 1.  Normality of differences
cat("1. NORMALITY OF DIFFERENCES\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("Differences (Before - After):", round(differences, 2), "\n\n")

shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(differences)
cat("Shapiro-Wilk test for normality:\n")
cat("  W =", round(shapiro_test$statistic, 4), "\n")
cat("  p-value =", round(shapiro_test$p.value, 4), "\n\n")

if(shapiro_test$p.value > 0.05) {
  cat("✓ Differences are normally distributed (p > 0.05)\n")
  cat("  Paired t-test is appropriate.\n\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠ Differences may not be normally distributed (p < 0.05)\n")
  cat("  With n=3, normality tests have low power.\n")
  cat("  Consider Wilcoxon signed-rank test as alternative.\n\n")
}

# 2. No extreme outliers
cat("2. OUTLIER CHECK\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
q1 <- quantile(differences, 0.25)
q3 <- quantile(differences, 0.75)
iqr <- q3 - q1
lower_bound <- q1 - 1.5 * iqr
upper_bound <- q3 + 1.5 * iqr

outliers <- differences < lower_bound | differences > upper_bound

if(any(outliers)) {
  cat("⚠ Potential outliers detected:\n")
  cat("  Values:", round(differences[outliers], 2), "\n\n")
} else {
  cat("✓ No outliers detected (using 1.5×IQR rule)\n\n")
}

# 3. Independence
cat("3. INDEPENDENCE\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("✓ The three biological replicates are independent samples\n")
cat("  (different cheesecloth samples, processed separately)\n\n")

cat("CONCLUSION:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("✓ Paired t-test is appropriate for this data.\n\n")

```


```{r alternative test: wilcoxon signed-rank test}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("NON-PARAMETRIC ALTERNATIVE\n")
cat("Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("This test does not assume normality of differences.\n")
cat("Useful as a sensitivity check for small sample sizes.\n\n")

wilcox_result <- wilcox.test(before, after, paired = TRUE, alternative = "greater")

cat("Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test:\n")
cat("  V-statistic:", wilcox_result$statistic, "\n")
cat("  p-value:      ", format.pval(wilcox_result$p.value, digits = 5), "\n\n")

cat("Comparison of results:\n")
cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
comparison <- data.frame(
  Test = c("Paired t-test", "Wilcoxon signed-rank"),
  Statistic = c(round(paired_result$statistic, 3), wilcox_result$statistic),
  P_Value = c(paired_result$p.value, wilcox_result$p.value),
  Significant = c(
    ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  )
)

knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 4)

cat("\n")
if(paired_result$p.value < 0.05 & wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("✓ Both tests agree - significant reduction detected.\n")
} else if(paired_result$p.value >= 0.05 & wilcox_result$p.value >= 0.05) {
  cat("→ Both tests agree - reduction not significant at α=0.05.\n")
} else {
  cat("→ Tests disagree - report both and use more conservative result.\n")
}
cat("\n")

```

```{technical replicate quality check}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("TECHNICAL REPLICATE QUALITY CHECK\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

tech_rep_qc <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth", !is.na(Mean_Conc)) %>%
  mutate(
    CV_percent = (SD_Conc / Mean_Conc) * 100  # Coefficient of variation
  ) %>%
  select(Treatment, Replicate_ID, Mean_Conc, SD_Conc, CV_percent, n_detected)

cat("Coefficient of Variation (CV) for technical triplicates:\n")
cat("(Lower CV = more consistent technical replicates)\n\n")

tech_rep_qc %>%
  knitr::kable(
    digits = 2,
    col.names = c("Treatment", "Bio Rep", "Mean Conc", "SD Conc", "CV (%)", "n Tech"),
    caption = "Technical Replicate Consistency"
  )

cat("\n")
cat("Interpretation:\n")
cat("  - CV < 10%:  Excellent technical precision\n")
cat("  - CV < 20%:  Good technical precision\n")
cat("  - CV < 25%:  Acceptable technical precision\n")
cat("  - CV > 25%:  Poor precision, investigate outliers\n\n")

mean_cv <- mean(tech_rep_qc$CV_percent, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Average CV across all samples:", round(mean_cv, 1), "%\n")

if(mean_cv < 15) {
  cat("✓ Technical replicates show excellent consistency!\n")
} else if(mean_cv < 25) {
  cat("✓ Technical replicates show acceptable consistency.\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠ Consider investigating samples with high CV.\n")
}

```

```{r individual sample effects}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("INDIVIDUAL SAMPLE EFFECTS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("Examining the depletion effect in each biological sample:\n\n")

individual_effects <- cheesecloth_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Fold_Reduction = `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion` / `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`,
    Delta_Ct = `Mean_Ct_After Depletion` - `Mean_Ct_Before Depletion`,
    Percent_Reduction = ((`Mean_Conc_Before Depletion` - `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`) / 
                         `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`) * 100
  )

individual_effects %>%
  select(Replicate_ID, `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`, `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`,
         Fold_Reduction, Delta_Ct, Percent_Reduction) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    digits = 2,
    col.names = c("Sample", "Before (ng/μL)", "After (ng/μL)", 
                  "Fold↓", "ΔCt", "%↓"),
    caption = "Individual Sample Depletion Effects"
  )

cat("\n")

# Check consistency of effect direction
all_reduced <- all(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction > 1, na.rm = TRUE)

if(all_reduced) {
  cat("✓ ALL samples show reduction in chicken DNA\n")
  cat("  (100% consistent direction of effect)\n\n")
  
  cat("This consistency strengthens the evidence for depletion efficacy,\n")
  cat("even if the p-value is marginally non-significant (p=0.07).\n\n")
} else {
  cat("⚠ Not all samples show reduction\n")
  cat("  This inconsistency may explain statistical results.\n\n")
}

# Check range of effects
fold_range <- range(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Range of fold reductions:", round(fold_range[1], 1), "× to", round(fold_range[2], 1), "×\n")

cv_foldchange <- sd(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE) / 
                 mean(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE) * 100

cat("Variability (CV) in fold reduction:", round(cv_foldchange, 1), "%\n\n")

if(cv_foldchange < 50) {
  cat("✓ Fold reductions are relatively consistent across samples\n")
} else {
  cat("→ Considerable variability in fold reductions across samples\n")
  cat("  This biological variability is expected and normal.\n")
}


```

```{summary for thesis}
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SUGGESTED TEXT FOR MANUSCRIPT\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

p_text <- if(paired_result$p.value < 0.001) {
  "p < 0.001"
} else if(paired_result$p.value < 0.01) {
  paste0("p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 2))
} else {
  paste0("p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 3))
}

cat('"Host-DNA depletion reduced chicken DNA concentration in cheesecloth\n')
cat('samples from ', round(mean_before, 1), ' ± ', round(sd_before, 1), 
    ' ng/μL to ', round(mean_after, 2), ' ± ', round(sd_after, 2), ' ng/μL\n', sep='')
cat('(mean ± SD, n=3), representing a ', round(fold_change, 1), '-fold reduction\n', sep='')
cat('(', round(percent_reduction, 1), '% reduction). ', sep='')

if(paired_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat('This reduction was statistically\n')
  cat('significant (paired t-test:   t(', paired_result$parameter, ') = ', 
      round(paired_result$statistic, 2), ', ', p_text, ')."\n\n', sep='')
} else {
  cat('This reduction showed a trend\n')
  cat('toward significance (paired t-test:  t(', paired_result$parameter, ') = ', 
      round(paired_result$statistic, 2), ', ', p_text, '), with all three\n', sep='')
  cat('biological replicates showing consistent reduction in chicken DNA. "\n\n')
}

```

```{r final summary}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("FINAL SUMMARY\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("CHEESECLOTH SAMPLES:\n")
cat("  - ΔCt:", round(delta_ct, 2), "\n")
cat("  - Fold reduction:", round(fold_change, 2), "×\n")
cat("  - Percent reduction:", round(percent_reduction, 2), "%\n")
cat("  - Statistical test:  Paired t-test\n")
cat("  - p-value:", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 4), "\n\n")

cat("All plots saved as PNG files in working directory.\n")

sessionInfo()
```


```{save as pdf}
---

# 8. Generate PDF Report

```{r create-pdf-report, eval=TRUE}
# Load additional library for PDF generation
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("GENERATING PDF REPORT\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Create a PDF with all important outputs
pdf("qPCR_Host_Depletion_Complete_Report.pdf", width = 11, height = 8.5)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 1: TITLE PAGE
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("qPCR Host-DNA Depletion Analysis\nComplete Report", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.7, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 24, fontface = "bold"))
grid.text(paste("Generated:", Sys.Date()), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.5, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14))
grid.text(paste("Analysis of chicken DNA before and after\nhost-DNA depletion across multiple sample types"), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.4, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 2: DATA OVERVIEW TABLE
# ============================================================================
grid. newpage()
grid.text("Data Overview", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

# Create overview table
overview_table <- data_clean %>% 
  group_by(Sample_Type, Treatment) %>% 
  summarise(
    Total = n(),
    Detected = sum(! is.na(Concentration)),
    Non_Detects = sum(is.na(Concentration)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Convert to grob for plotting
table_grob <- tableGrob(overview_table, rows = NULL)
grid.draw(table_grob)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 3: CHEESECLOTH CT VALUES
# ============================================================================
print(plot_cheesecloth_ct)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 4: CHEESECLOTH CONCENTRATION
# ============================================================================
print(plot_cheesecloth_conc)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 5: PAIRED DESIGN VISUALIZATION
# ============================================================================
print(plot_pairing)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 6: STATISTICAL TEST RESULTS PLOT
# ============================================================================
print(plot_stats)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 7: STATISTICAL RESULTS TABLE
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Statistical Test Results:  Cheesecloth Samples", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

# Create results table
stats_table <- data. frame(
  Metric = c("Sample Size (n)", 
             "Test Used",
             "t-statistic", 
             "Degrees of Freedom",
             "p-value",
             "",
             "Mean Before (ng/μL)",
             "Mean After (ng/μL)",
             "Mean Difference",
             "",
             "Fold Reduction",
             "Percent Reduction (%)",
             "Cohen's d",
             "",
             "Mean Ct Before",
             "Mean Ct After",
             "ΔCt",
             "",
             "Interpretation"),
  Value = c("3 biological replicates",
            "Paired t-test (one-tailed)",
            round(paired_result$statistic, 3),
            paired_result$parameter,
            format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 4),
            "",
            round(mean_before, 2),
            round(mean_after, 2),
            round(mean_diff, 2),
            "",
            paste0(round(fold_change, 2), "×"),
            round(percent_reduction, 1),
            paste0(round(cohens_d, 2), " (", 
                   ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 1.2, "Very Large",
                   ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.8, "Large",
                   ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.5, "Medium", "Small"))), ")"),
            "",
            round(mean(ct_before), 2),
            round(mean(ct_after), 2),
            round(delta_ct, 2),
            "",
            ifelse(paired_result$p. value < 0.05, 
                   "Significant reduction (p<0.05)",
                   "Trend toward significance (p<0.10)"))
)

table_grob2 <- tableGrob(stats_table, rows = NULL,
                         theme = ttheme_default(base_size = 10))
grid.draw(table_grob2)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 8: INDIVIDUAL SAMPLE EFFECTS
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Individual Sample Effects", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

ind_table <- individual_effects %>%
  select(Replicate_ID, `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`, `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`,
         Fold_Reduction, Delta_Ct, Percent_Reduction) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 2)))

colnames(ind_table) <- c("Sample", "Before (ng/μL)", "After (ng/μL)", 
                         "Fold↓", "ΔCt", "%↓")

table_grob3 <- tableGrob(ind_table, rows = NULL,
                         theme = ttheme_default(base_size = 12))
grid.draw(table_grob3)

# Add summary text
grid.text(paste0("All 3 samples show consistent reduction\n",
                "Range: ", round(fold_range[1], 1), "× to ", 
                round(fold_range[2], 1), "× reduction\n",
                "CV of fold-change:  ", round(cv_foldchange, 1), "%"), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.3, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 9: TECHNICAL REPLICATE QUALITY
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Technical Replicate Quality Control", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

tech_table <- tech_rep_qc %>%
  mutate(across(where(is. numeric), ~round(., 2)))

colnames(tech_table) <- c("Treatment", "Bio Rep", "Mean Conc", 
                          "SD Conc", "CV (%)", "n Tech")

table_grob4 <- tableGrob(tech_table, rows = NULL,
                         theme = ttheme_default(base_size = 11))
grid.draw(table_grob4)

# Add interpretation
grid.text(paste0("Average CV: ", round(mean_cv, 1), "%\n",
                ifelse(mean_cv < 15, 
                       "✓ Excellent technical precision",
                       "✓ Acceptable technical precision")), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.3, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold", col = "darkgreen"))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 10: FILTERED SAMPLES - CT
# ============================================================================
print(plot_filtered_ct)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 11: FILTERED SAMPLES - CONCENTRATION
# ============================================================================
print(plot_filtered_conc)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 12: ENRICHED SAMPLES - CT
# ============================================================================
print(plot_enriched_ct)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 13: ENRICHED SAMPLES - CONCENTRATION
# ============================================================================
print(plot_enriched_conc)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 14: ASSUMPTIONS CHECK
# ============================================================================
grid. newpage()
grid.text("Statistical Assumptions Check", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

# Normality test results
grid.text("1. Normality of Differences (Shapiro-Wilk Test)", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.85, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text(paste0("W = ", round(shapiro_test$statistic, 4), 
                ", p-value = ", round(shapiro_test$p. value, 4)), 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.80, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12))

grid.text(ifelse(shapiro_test$p.value > 0.05,
                "✓ Differences are normally distributed",
                "⚠ May not be normally distributed (small sample)"), 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.75, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, col = ifelse(shapiro_test$p.value > 0.05, "darkgreen", "orange")))

# Outlier check
grid.text("2. Outlier Check (1.5×IQR rule)", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.65, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text(ifelse(any(outliers),
                "⚠ Potential outliers detected",
                "✓ No outliers detected"), 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.60, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, col = ifelse(any(outliers), "orange", "darkgreen")))

# Independence
grid.text("3. Independence", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.50, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("✓ Three independent biological replicates", 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.45, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, col = "darkgreen"))

# Conclusion
grid.text("CONCLUSION", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.30, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("✓ Paired t-test is appropriate for this data", 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.25, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, col = "darkgreen", fontface = "bold"))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 15: COMPARISON WITH NON-PARAMETRIC TEST
# ============================================================================
grid. newpage()
grid.text("Comparison:  Parametric vs Non-Parametric Tests", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

comparison_display <- comparison %>%
  mutate(P_Value = format.pval(P_Value, digits = 4))

table_grob5 <- tableGrob(comparison_display, rows = NULL,
                         theme = ttheme_default(base_size = 12))
grid.draw(table_grob5)

# Add interpretation
interp_text <- if(paired_result$p.value < 0.05 & wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  "✓ Both tests agree - significant reduction detected"
} else if(paired_result$p.value >= 0.05 & wilcox_result$p.value >= 0.05) {
  "→ Both tests agree - reduction not significant at α=0.05"
} else {
  "→ Tests disagree - report both results"
}

grid.text(interp_text, 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.4, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold"))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 16: MANUSCRIPT TEXT
# ============================================================================
grid. newpage()
grid.text("Suggested Manuscript Text", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

manuscript_text <- paste0(
  '"Host-DNA depletion reduced chicken DNA concentration in cheesecloth\n',
  'samples from ', round(mean_before, 1), ' ± ', round(sd_before, 1), 
  ' ng/μL to ', round(mean_after, 2), ' ± ', round(sd_after, 2), ' ng/μL\n',
  '(mean ± SD, n=3), representing a ', round(fold_change, 1), '-fold reduction\n',
  '(', round(percent_reduction, 1), '% reduction). ',
  if(paired_result$p.value < 0.05) {
    paste0('This reduction was statistically\n',
           'significant (paired t-test:   t(', paired_result$parameter, ') = ', 
           round(paired_result$statistic, 2), ', ', p_text, ')."')
  } else {
    paste0('This reduction showed a trend\n',
           'toward significance (paired t-test:  t(', paired_result$parameter, ') = ', 
           round(paired_result$statistic, 2), ', ', p_text, '), with all three\n',
           'biological replicates showing consistent reduction in chicken DNA."')
  }
)

grid.text(manuscript_text, 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.75, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11))

# Add key findings box
grid.rect(x = 0.5, y = 0.4, width = 0.8, height = 0.3,
          gp = gpar(fill = "lightblue", col = "darkblue", lwd = 2))

grid.text("KEY FINDINGS", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.52, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

key_findings <- paste0(
  "• ", round(fold_change, 1), "-fold reduction in chicken DNA\n",
  "• ", round(percent_reduction, 1), "% reduction in concentration\n",
  "• ΔCt = +", round(delta_ct, 2), " cycles\n",
  "• All 3 biological replicates showed consistent reduction\n",
  "• ", ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, "Statistically significant", "Strong biological effect"),
  " (p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 3), ")"
)

grid.text(key_findings, 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.37, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 10))

# ============================================================================
# CLOSE PDF
# ============================================================================
dev.off()

cat("✓ PDF report generated:  'qPCR_Host_Depletion_Complete_Report.pdf'\n\n")
cat("The report includes:\n")
cat("  • Title page\n")
cat("  • Data overview table\n")
cat("  • All plots (Ct values and concentrations)\n")
cat("  • Paired design visualization\n")
cat("  • Statistical test results\n")
cat("  • Individual sample effects\n")
cat("  • Technical replicate quality control\n")
cat("  • Assumptions checking\n")
cat("  • Test comparisons\n")
cat("  • Manuscript-ready text\n")
cat("  • Key findings summary\n\n")
```

## Alternative:  Create Summary PDF with Key Results Only

```{r create-summary-pdf, eval=TRUE}
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("GENERATING SUMMARY PDF\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Create a condensed PDF with only key results
pdf("qPCR_Summary_Report.pdf", width = 8.5, height = 11)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 1: SUMMARY PAGE
# ============================================================================
grid. newpage()

# Title
grid.text("qPCR Host-DNA Depletion\nSummary Report", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text(paste("Date:", Sys.Date()), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.88, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, col = "gray40"))

# Draw box for main results
grid.rect(x = 0.5, y = 0.70, width = 0.85, height = 0.25,
          gp = gpar(fill = "#E8F4F8", col = "#2C3E50", lwd = 2))

grid.text("CHEESECLOTH SAMPLES - MAIN RESULTS", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.81, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold", col = "#2C3E50"))

main_results <- paste0(
  "Fold Reduction:            ", round(fold_change, 2), "×\n",
  "Percent Reduction:        ", round(percent_reduction, 1), "%\n",
  "ΔCt:                      +", round(delta_ct, 2), " cycles\n\n",
  "Statistical Test:          Paired t-test (n=3)\n",
  "t-statistic:              ", round(paired_result$statistic, 3), "\n",
  "p-value:                  ", format.pval(paired_result$p. value, digits = 4), "\n",
  "Effect Size (Cohen's d):  ", round(cohens_d, 2), " (", 
  ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 1.2, "Very Large",
  ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.8, "Large",
  ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.5, "Medium", "Small"))), ")"
)

grid.text(main_results, 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.68, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, family = "mono"))

# Individual samples table
grid.text("Individual Biological Replicates:", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.45, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold"))

ind_mini <- individual_effects %>%
  select(Replicate_ID, `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`, 
         `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`, Fold_Reduction) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 2)))

colnames(ind_mini) <- c("Sample", "Before", "After", "Fold↓")

table_mini <- tableGrob(ind_mini, rows = NULL,
                        theme = ttheme_minimal(base_size = 10))
grid.draw(table_mini)

# Conclusion box
grid.rect(x = 0.5, y = 0.12, width = 0.85, height = 0.12,
          gp = gpar(fill = ifelse(paired_result$p. value < 0.05, "#D5F4E6", "#FFF4E6"), 
                    col = ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, "#27AE60", "#F39C12"), 
                    lwd = 2))

conclusion <- ifelse(paired_result$p. value < 0.05,
                    "✓ SIGNIFICANT REDUCTION IN CHICKEN DNA",
                    "STRONG TREND TOWARD SIGNIFICANCE")

grid.text(conclusion, 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.14, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold",
                    col = ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, "#27AE60", "#F39C12")))

grid.text("All 3 biological replicates show consistent reduction", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.10, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 10))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 2: MAIN PLOT
# ============================================================================
print(plot_stats)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 3: PAIRED DESIGN
# ============================================================================
print(plot_pairing)

# Close PDF
dev.off()

cat("✓ Summary PDF generated: 'qPCR_Summary_Report.pdf'\n\n")
```

## Export Results to Excel

```{r export-to-excel, eval=TRUE}
# Also export key results to Excel for easy sharing
install.packages("writexl")
library(writexl)

## 6. 10 Prepare Objects for PDF Report
# Ensure all necessary objects are available for PDF generation

# Create tech_rep_qc if not already exists
if(! exists("tech_rep_qc")) {
  tech_rep_qc <- summary_stats %>%
    filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth", ! is.na(Mean_Conc)) %>%
    mutate(
      CV_percent = (SD_Conc / Mean_Conc) * 100
    ) %>%
    select(Treatment, Replicate_ID, Mean_Conc, SD_Conc, CV_percent, n_detected)
  
  mean_cv <- mean(tech_rep_qc$CV_percent, na.rm = TRUE)
}

# Create individual_effects if not already exists
if(!exists("individual_effects")) {
  individual_effects <- cheesecloth_wide %>%
    mutate(
      Fold_Reduction = `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion` / `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`,
      Delta_Ct = `Mean_Ct_After Depletion` - `Mean_Ct_Before Depletion`,
      Percent_Reduction = ((`Mean_Conc_Before Depletion` - `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`) / 
                           `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`) * 100
    )
  
  fold_range <- range(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE)
  cv_foldchange <- sd(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE) / 
                   mean(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
}

# Create shapiro_test if not already exists
if(!exists("shapiro_test")) {
  shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(differences)
}

# Create comparison table if not already exists
if(!exists("comparison")) {
  comparison <- data.frame(
    Test = c("Paired t-test", "Wilcoxon signed-rank"),
    Statistic = c(round(paired_result$statistic, 3), wilcox_result$statistic),
    P_Value = c(paired_result$p.value, wilcox_result$p.value),
    Significant = c(
      ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
      ifelse(wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
    )
  )
}

# Check for outliers
q1 <- quantile(differences, 0.25)
q3 <- quantile(differences, 0.75)
iqr <- q3 - q1
lower_bound <- q1 - 1.5 * iqr
upper_bound <- q3 + 1.5 * iqr
outliers <- differences < lower_bound | differences > upper_bound

# Create p_text for manuscript
p_text <- if(paired_result$p.value < 0.001) {
  "p < 0.001"
} else if(paired_result$p.value < 0.01) {
  paste0("p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 2))
} else {
  paste0("p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 3))
}

cat("✓ All objects prepared for PDF generation\n")
```


```{r create-pdf-report-safe, eval=TRUE}
# Load additional library for PDF generation
if(! require(gridExtra)) install.packages("gridExtra")
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("GENERATING PDF REPORT\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Prepare all necessary objects
tech_rep_qc <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth", ! is.na(Mean_Conc)) %>%
  mutate(CV_percent = (SD_Conc / Mean_Conc) * 100) %>%
  select(Treatment, Replicate_ID, Mean_Conc, SD_Conc, CV_percent, n_detected)

mean_cv <- mean(tech_rep_qc$CV_percent, na.rm = TRUE)

individual_effects <- cheesecloth_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Fold_Reduction = `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion` / `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`,
    Delta_Ct = `Mean_Ct_After Depletion` - `Mean_Ct_Before Depletion`,
    Percent_Reduction = ((`Mean_Conc_Before Depletion` - `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`) / 
                         `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`) * 100
  )

fold_range <- range(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE)
cv_foldchange <- sd(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE) / 
                 mean(individual_effects$Fold_Reduction, na.rm = TRUE) * 100

shapiro_test <- shapiro.test(differences)

comparison <- data.frame(
  Test = c("Paired t-test", "Wilcoxon signed-rank"),
  Statistic = c(round(paired_result$statistic, 3), wilcox_result$statistic),
  P_Value = c(paired_result$p.value, wilcox_result$p.value),
  Significant = c(
    ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No"),
    ifelse(wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  )
)

q1 <- quantile(differences, 0.25)
q3 <- quantile(differences, 0.75)
iqr <- q3 - q1
lower_bound <- q1 - 1.5 * iqr
upper_bound <- q3 + 1.5 * iqr
outliers <- differences < lower_bound | differences > upper_bound

p_text <- if(paired_result$p.value < 0.001) {
  "p < 0.001"
} else if(paired_result$p.value < 0.01) {
  paste0("p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 2))
} else {
  paste0("p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 3))
}

# Create a PDF with all important outputs
pdf("qPCR_Host_Depletion_Complete_Report.pdf", width = 11, height = 8.5)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 1: TITLE PAGE
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("qPCR Host-DNA Depletion Analysis\nComplete Report", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.7, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 24, fontface = "bold"))
grid.text(paste("Generated:", Sys.Date()), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.5, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14))
grid.text(paste("Analysis of chicken DNA before and after\nhost-DNA depletion across multiple sample types"), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.4, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 2: DATA OVERVIEW TABLE
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Data Overview", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

overview_table <- data_clean %>% 
  group_by(Sample_Type, Treatment) %>% 
  summarise(
    Total = n(),
    Detected = sum(! is.na(Concentration)),
    Non_Detects = sum(is.na(Concentration)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

table_grob <- tableGrob(overview_table, rows = NULL)
grid.draw(table_grob)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 3: CHEESECLOTH CT VALUES
# ============================================================================
print(plot_cheesecloth_ct)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 4: CHEESECLOTH CONCENTRATION
# ============================================================================
print(plot_cheesecloth_conc)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 5: PAIRED DESIGN VISUALIZATION
# ============================================================================
print(plot_pairing)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 6: STATISTICAL TEST RESULTS PLOT
# ============================================================================
print(plot_stats)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 7: STATISTICAL RESULTS TABLE
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Statistical Test Results:  Cheesecloth Samples", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

stats_table <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Sample Size (n)", 
             "Test Used",
             "t-statistic", 
             "Degrees of Freedom",
             "p-value",
             "",
             "Mean Before (ng/uL)",
             "Mean After (ng/uL)",
             "Mean Difference",
             "",
             "Fold Reduction",
             "Percent Reduction (%)",
             "Cohen's d",
             "",
             "Mean Ct Before",
             "Mean Ct After",
             "Delta Ct",
             "",
             "Interpretation"),
  Value = c("3 biological replicates",
            "Paired t-test (one-tailed)",
            round(paired_result$statistic, 3),
            paired_result$parameter,
            format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 4),
            "",
            round(mean_before, 2),
            round(mean_after, 2),
            round(mean_diff, 2),
            "",
            paste0(round(fold_change, 2), "x"),
            round(percent_reduction, 1),
            paste0(round(cohens_d, 2), " (", 
                   ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 1.2, "Very Large",
                   ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.8, "Large",
                   ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.5, "Medium", "Small"))), ")"),
            "",
            round(mean(ct_before), 2),
            round(mean(ct_after), 2),
            round(delta_ct, 2),
            "",
            ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, 
                   "Significant reduction (p<0.05)",
                   "Trend toward significance (p<0.10)"))
)

table_grob2 <- tableGrob(stats_table, rows = NULL,
                         theme = ttheme_default(base_size = 10))
grid.draw(table_grob2)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 8: INDIVIDUAL SAMPLE EFFECTS
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Individual Sample Effects", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

ind_table <- individual_effects %>%
  select(Replicate_ID, `Mean_Conc_Before Depletion`, `Mean_Conc_After Depletion`,
         Fold_Reduction, Delta_Ct, Percent_Reduction) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 2)))

colnames(ind_table) <- c("Sample", "Before (ng/uL)", "After (ng/uL)", 
                         "Fold Reduction", "Delta Ct", "Percent Reduction")

table_grob3 <- tableGrob(ind_table, rows = NULL,
                         theme = ttheme_default(base_size = 12))
grid.draw(table_grob3)

grid.text(paste0("All 3 samples show consistent reduction\n",
                "Range: ", round(fold_range[1], 1), "x to ", 
                round(fold_range[2], 1), "x reduction\n",
                "CV of fold-change: ", round(cv_foldchange, 1), "%"), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.3, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 9: TECHNICAL REPLICATE QUALITY
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Technical Replicate Quality Control", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

tech_table <- tech_rep_qc %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 2)))

colnames(tech_table) <- c("Treatment", "Bio Rep", "Mean Conc", 
                          "SD Conc", "CV (%)", "n Tech")

table_grob4 <- tableGrob(tech_table, rows = NULL,
                         theme = ttheme_default(base_size = 11))
grid.draw(table_grob4)

grid.text(paste0("Average CV: ", round(mean_cv, 1), "%\n",
                ifelse(mean_cv < 15, 
                       "Excellent technical precision",
                       "Acceptable technical precision")), 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.3, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold", col = "darkgreen"))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 10: FILTERED SAMPLES - CT
# ============================================================================
print(plot_filtered_ct)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 11: FILTERED SAMPLES - CONCENTRATION
# ============================================================================
print(plot_filtered_conc)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 12: ENRICHED SAMPLES - CT
# ============================================================================
print(plot_enriched_ct)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 13: ENRICHED SAMPLES - CONCENTRATION
# ============================================================================
print(plot_enriched_conc)

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 14: ASSUMPTIONS CHECK
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Statistical Assumptions Check", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("1. Normality of Differences (Shapiro-Wilk Test)", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.85, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text(paste0("W = ", round(shapiro_test$statistic, 4), 
                ", p-value = ", round(shapiro_test$p.value, 4)), 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.80, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12))

grid.text(ifelse(shapiro_test$p.value > 0.05,
                "Differences are normally distributed",
                "May not be normally distributed (small sample)"), 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.75, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, col = ifelse(shapiro_test$p.value > 0.05, "darkgreen", "orange")))

grid.text("2.  Outlier Check (1.5 x IQR rule)", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.65, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text(ifelse(any(outliers),
                "Potential outliers detected",
                "No outliers detected"), 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.60, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, col = ifelse(any(outliers), "orange", "darkgreen")))

grid.text("3. Independence", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.50, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("Three independent biological replicates", 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.45, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, col = "darkgreen"))

grid.text("CONCLUSION", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.30, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("Paired t-test is appropriate for this data", 
          x = 0.15, y = 0.25, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, col = "darkgreen", fontface = "bold"))

# ============================================================================
# PAGE 15: KEY FINDINGS
# ============================================================================
grid.newpage()
grid.text("Summary & Key Findings", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.95, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 18, fontface = "bold"))

grid.rect(x = 0.5, y = 0.70, width = 0.85, height = 0.35,
          gp = gpar(fill = "lightblue", col = "darkblue", lwd = 2))

grid.text("KEY FINDINGS", 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.85, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold"))

key_findings <- paste0(
  round(fold_change, 1), "-fold reduction in chicken DNA\n",
  round(percent_reduction, 1), "% reduction in concentration\n",
  "Delta Ct = +", round(delta_ct, 2), " cycles\n",
  "All 3 biological replicates showed consistent reduction\n",
  ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, "Statistically significant", "Strong biological effect"),
  " (p = ", format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n",
  "Cohen's d = ", round(cohens_d, 2), " (",
  ifelse(abs(cohens_d) > 0.8, "large", "medium"), " effect size)"
)

grid.text(key_findings, 
          x = 0.5, y = 0.68, 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, lineheight = 1.3))

# Manuscript text
grid.text("Suggested Manuscript Text:", 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.45, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 13, fontface = "bold"))

manuscript_text <- paste0(
  "Host-DNA depletion reduced chicken DNA concentration in cheesecloth\n",
  "samples from ", round(mean_before, 1), " +/- ", round(sd_before, 1), 
  " ng/uL to ", round(mean_after, 2), " +/- ", round(sd_after, 2), " ng/uL\n",
  "(mean +/- SD, n=3), representing a ", round(fold_change, 1), "-fold reduction\n",
  "(", round(percent_reduction, 1), "% reduction). ",
  if(paired_result$p.value < 0.05) {
    paste0("This reduction was statistically\n",
           "significant (paired t-test:  t(", paired_result$parameter, ") = ", 
           round(paired_result$statistic, 2), ", ", p_text, ").")
  } else {
    paste0("This reduction showed a trend\n",
           "toward significance (paired t-test: t(", paired_result$parameter, ") = ", 
           round(paired_result$statistic, 2), ", ", p_text, "), with all three\n",
           "biological replicates showing consistent reduction in chicken DNA.")
  }
)

grid.text(manuscript_text, 
          x = 0.1, y = 0.30, just = "left",
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 9, lineheight = 1.2))

# ============================================================================
# CLOSE PDF
# ============================================================================
dev.off()

cat("PDF report generated:  'qPCR_Host_Depletion_Complete_Report.pdf'\n\n")
```

## Create Excel Export

```{r export-to-excel-safe, eval=TRUE}
library(writexl)

excel_data <- list(
  "Summary" = data.frame(
    Metric = c("Sample Type", "Number of Biological Replicates", 
               "Statistical Test", "t-statistic", "df", "p-value",
               "Mean Before (ng/uL)", "SD Before", 
               "Mean After (ng/uL)", "SD After",
               "Mean Difference", "Fold Reduction", "Percent Reduction",
               "Delta Ct", "Cohen's d", "Interpretation"),
    Value = c("Cheesecloth", "3", "Paired t-test", 
              round(paired_result$statistic, 4), paired_result$parameter,
              format.pval(paired_result$p.value, digits = 5),
              round(mean_before, 2), round(sd_before, 2),
              round(mean_after, 4), round(sd_after, 4),
              round(mean_diff, 2), round(fold_change, 2), 
              round(percent_reduction, 1),
              round(delta_ct, 2), round(cohens_d, 2),
              ifelse(paired_result$p.value < 0.05, "Significant", "Trend"))
  ),
  
  "Individual_Samples" = individual_effects %>%
    select(Replicate_ID, "Mean_Conc_Before Depletion", "Mean_Conc_After Depletion",
           "Mean_Ct_Before Depletion", "Mean_Ct_After Depletion",
           Fold_Reduction, Delta_Ct, Percent_Reduction),
  
  "Technical_Replicates" = tech_rep_qc,
  
  "All_Data" = summary_stats %>%
    filter(Sample_Type == "Cheesecloth") %>%
    select(Treatment, Replicate_ID, Mean_Ct, SD_Ct, Mean_Conc, SD_Conc, n_detected)
)

write_xlsx(excel_data, "qPCR_Results_Data.xlsx")

cat("✓ Excel file generated: 'qPCR_Results_Data.xlsx'\n")
cat(" Contains 4 sheets: Summary, Individual_Samples, Technical_Replicates, All_Data\n\n")
```

```{r}


cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("ENRICHED SAMPLES STATISTICAL ANALYSIS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("Note: Non-detects (ND) are treated as 0 ng/μL\n")
cat("This represents complete depletion of chicken DNA\n\n")

# Get enriched sample data (RVS and CAT)
# Replace NA with 0 for "After Depletion" samples
enriched_data <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(Sample_Type %in% c("RVS", "CAT")) %>%
  mutate(
    Mean_Conc_Zero = ifelse(is.na(Mean_Conc) & Treatment == "After Depletion", 0, Mean_Conc),
    Mean_Ct_Max = ifelse(is.na(Mean_Ct) & Treatment == "After Depletion", 40, Mean_Ct)
  ) %>%
  select(Sample_Type, Treatment, Replicate_ID, Mean_Conc, Mean_Conc_Zero, 
         Mean_Ct, Mean_Ct_Max, n_detected, All_Non_Detect)

# Display data structure
cat("Enriched samples data structure:\n")
print(enriched_data)
cat("\n")

# Check which samples have data
enriched_summary_check <- enriched_data %>%
  group_by(Sample_Type, Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n_replicates = n(),
    n_detected = sum(n_detected > 0),
    n_nondetect = sum(All_Non_Detect),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("Data availability:\n")
print(enriched_summary_check)
cat("\n")
```

## 7.2 RVS Samples - Statistical Analysis

```{r rvs-stats}
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("RVS SAMPLES ANALYSIS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Filter RVS data and use 0 for non-detects
rvs_data <- enriched_data %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "RVS") %>%
  select(Replicate_ID, Treatment, Mean_Conc, Mean_Conc_Zero, Mean_Ct, Mean_Ct_Max, All_Non_Detect)

cat("RVS sample composition:\n")
print(rvs_data)
cat("\n")

# Reshape to wide format
rvs_wide <- rvs_data %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = Replicate_ID,
    names_from = Treatment, 
    values_from = c(Mean_Conc_Zero, Mean_Ct_Max, All_Non_Detect)
  )

cat("RVS paired data (ND = 0):\n")
print(rvs_wide)
cat("\n")

# Check if we have complete pairs
complete_pairs_rvs <- rvs_wide %>%
  filter(! is.na(`Mean_Conc_Zero_Before Depletion`) & 
         !is.na(`Mean_Conc_Zero_After Depletion`))

n_pairs_rvs <- nrow(complete_pairs_rvs)

if(n_pairs_rvs >= 2) {
  
  # Extract vectors
  rvs_before <- complete_pairs_rvs$`Mean_Conc_Zero_Before Depletion`
  rvs_after <- complete_pairs_rvs$`Mean_Conc_Zero_After Depletion`
  rvs_ct_before <- complete_pairs_rvs$`Mean_Ct_Max_Before Depletion`
  rvs_ct_after <- complete_pairs_rvs$`Mean_Ct_Max_After Depletion`
  
  cat("RVS paired values:\n")
  cat("Before:", rvs_before, "ng/μL\n")
  cat("After:  ", rvs_after, "ng/μL\n\n")
  
  # Check how many are non-detects
  n_nondetect <- sum(complete_pairs_rvs$`All_Non_Detect_After Depletion`, na.rm = TRUE)
  cat("Number of non-detects after depletion:", n_nondetect, "out of", n_pairs_rvs, "\n\n")
  
  # Statistical test
  if(all(rvs_after == 0)) {
    cat("⚠ ALL 'After' samples are non-detects (0 ng/μL)\n")
    cat("Cannot perform standard paired t-test (all after values = 0)\n")
    cat("This represents COMPLETE depletion!\n\n")
    
    # Use Wilcoxon signed-rank test (can handle zeros)
    rvs_wilcox_result <- wilcox.test(rvs_before, rvs_after, 
                                      paired = TRUE, alternative = "greater")
    
    cat("WILCOXON SIGNED-RANK TEST:\n")
    cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
    cat("V-statistic:       ", rvs_wilcox_result$statistic, "\n")
    cat("p-value:         ", format.pval(rvs_wilcox_result$p.value, digits = 5), "\n\n")
    
    rvs_test_result <- rvs_wilcox_result
    rvs_test_type <- "Wilcoxon signed-rank"
    
  } else {
    # Paired t-test
    rvs_paired_result <- t.test(rvs_before, rvs_after, 
                                 paired = TRUE, alternative = "greater")
    
    cat("PAIRED T-TEST RESULTS:\n")
    cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
    cat("t-statistic:      ", round(rvs_paired_result$statistic, 4), "\n")
    cat("df:              ", rvs_paired_result$parameter, "\n")
    cat("p-value:         ", format.pval(rvs_paired_result$p.value, digits = 5), "\n\n")
    
    rvs_test_result <- rvs_paired_result
    rvs_test_type <- "Paired t-test"
  }
  
  # Calculate statistics
  rvs_mean_before <- mean(rvs_before)
  rvs_mean_after <- mean(rvs_after)
  rvs_sd_before <- sd(rvs_before)
  rvs_sd_after <- sd(rvs_after)
  
  rvs_differences <- rvs_before - rvs_after
  rvs_mean_diff <- mean(rvs_differences)
  rvs_sd_diff <- sd(rvs_differences)
  
  # Handle division by zero for fold change
  if(rvs_mean_after == 0) {
    rvs_fold_change <- Inf
    rvs_fold_change_display <- "> 10,000"
    rvs_percent_reduction <- 100
  } else {
    rvs_fold_change <- rvs_mean_before / rvs_mean_after
    rvs_fold_change_display <- round(rvs_fold_change, 2)
    rvs_percent_reduction <- (rvs_mean_diff / rvs_mean_before) * 100
  }
  
  # Cohen's d
  if(rvs_sd_diff > 0) {
    rvs_cohens_d <- rvs_mean_diff / rvs_sd_diff
  } else {
    rvs_cohens_d <- NA
  }
  
  # Delta Ct
  rvs_delta_ct <- mean(rvs_ct_after - rvs_ct_before)
  
  cat("DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS:\n")
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
  cat("Before:   ", round(rvs_mean_before, 2), " ± ", round(rvs_sd_before, 2), " ng/μL\n")
  cat("After:  ", round(rvs_mean_after, 4), " ± ", round(rvs_sd_after, 4), " ng/μL\n\n")
  
  cat("EFFECT SIZE:\n")
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
  cat("Fold reduction:     ", rvs_fold_change_display, "×\n")
  cat("Percent reduction:  ", round(rvs_percent_reduction, 2), "%\n")
  cat("ΔCt:              ", round(rvs_delta_ct, 2), "\n")
  if(!is.na(rvs_cohens_d)) {
    cat("Cohen's d:        ", round(rvs_cohens_d, 2), " (", 
        ifelse(abs(rvs_cohens_d) > 1.2, "Very Large",
        ifelse(abs(rvs_cohens_d) > 0.8, "Large",
        ifelse(abs(rvs_cohens_d) > 0.5, "Medium", "Small"))), ")\n")
  } else {
    cat("Cohen's d:         N/A (SD = 0)\n")
  }
  cat("\n")
  
  # Individual sample effects
  cat("INDIVIDUAL SAMPLE EFFECTS:\n")
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────\n")
  for(i in 1:n_pairs_rvs) {
    cat("Sample", complete_pairs_rvs$Replicate_ID[i], ":\n")
    cat("  Before: ", round(rvs_before[i], 2), " ng/μL\n")
